etymology.description: phonetic 麻 (má) + semantic 幺 (small, tiny) -> question/softening particle
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 幺(幺) (yāo, "small, tiny") referring to something minor or subtle, with the phonetic 麻(麻) (má, "hemp"), to create a particle used to denote questions or emphasis, perhaps implying a subtle inquiry or emphasis.
etymology.simplification: The simplification involved reducing the character size and complexity by employing 幺(幺) (yāo, "small") directly to form the modern simplified 么.

examples.chinese: 你要去哪里么(你要去哪裡麼)？
这件事你知道么(這件事你知道麼)？
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn shì nǐ zhīdào me?

examples.chinese: 这花很漂亮么(這花很漂亮麼)！
